metadata.title:
c. P. To EXTEND


To SOUND

pages.raw_text:
SUMAS, Wash, Sept. 13.-1t is
admitted by surveyors who for the
past two months have been work
ing southwark from this city that
the Canadian Pacific will, before
the end of the year, begin work
on a line to invade the Sound
country.


The plan is to parallel the line
of the Northern Pacific from here
to Sedro Woolley, in Skagit county
but the route from there to Everett
has not been determined. The in
tention is to get right into the ter
ritory of the Hill roads and re
taliate promptly for the Invasion
bv Hill of Canadian territory.


In this connection there is some
significance in the company's i'm
portation of Asiatic laborers. The
Japanese from Honolulu have beer
brought over on contract with the
Canadian Pacific, and since the rio
in Vancouver the railroad compan!
has offered to employ all the sur
plus Hindus. If the Canadian Pa
cific desires TO begin construction
of the Washington extension in thu
near future it could gather quit
an army for work on grading.
